# mobrpg CLI — guide for AI agents

`mobrpg` is a CLI over the mobRPG world-builder REST API (Tim Dennis'
mobrpg.com), used to sync mobRPG worlds with gm-apprentice campaign vaults.
It is a strangler-stage tool: some verbs are native Python subcommands, the
rest shell out to the original prototype scripts in this directory. All verbs
share one client (mobrpg/client.py) for environment resolution and auth.

## Install

    python3 -m pip install -e path/to/docs/prototypes/mobrpg
    mobrpg --help

## Environment & auth (read before running anything)

- MOBRPG_ENV=dev|prod   Which server to hit. Default: prod. The resolved
                        target (env + BASE URL) is printed to stderr on every
                        run — never ambiguous.
- MOBRPG_TOKEN          Bearer token (recommended). Authenticates as the token
                        owner. Treat like a password.
- MOBRPG_EMAIL / MOBRPG_PASSWORD   Alternative to a bearer token (local-password
                        accounts only). Works against either environment.
- MOBRPG_ALLOW_PROD_WRITES=1   REQUIRED, in addition to MOBRPG_ENV=prod, for any
                        mutating verb (push, suggest, types, links) to touch
                        production. Without it, writes to prod refuse and exit 3.
- Optional per-field overrides: MOBRPG_BASE, MOBRPG_CLIENT_ID, MOBRPG_REDIRECT_URI.

Exit codes: 0 ok; 1 API error; 2 bad args / no auth configured; 3 refused prod
write (missing MOBRPG_ALLOW_PROD_WRITES).

## Read-only vs mutating

Read-only (safe anywhere): whoami, worlds, pull, suggestions, catalog, map, sync, images.
Mutating (need write access; on prod also need MOBRPG_ALLOW_PROD_WRITES=1):
push, suggest, suggest-desc, submit-batch, update, types, links, review. write/merge/
link-orphans/pull-canon/pull-desc/backfill/relink only touch local vault files,
not the API (pull-canon and pull-desc read from mobRPG but only ever write
locally; backfill and relink make no API calls at all).

## Commands

    mobrpg whoami                 Print the authenticated user and their worlds.
    mobrpg worlds                 (same as whoami) list visible worlds.
    mobrpg pull <world> [--out F] Import a mobRPG world into a JSON extract
                                  (default extract.json). Entry of the import
                                  pipeline; feeds `write`. The extract carries
                                  entities/events/counts plus a `types` section
                                  (creature/organization/political classifier
                                  types, bound or not) for "what's new" discovery.
    mobrpg suggestions <world> [--state Pending|Accepted|Dismissed] [--correlate]
                       [--vault DIR] [--fetch-elements] [--json] [--out DIR]
                                  List suggestions by review state (the return
                                  leg of `suggest`). --correlate joins each back
                                  to its vault file (externalRef) and shows the
                                  resultElementId an Accepted one produced. Needs
                                  ReadWriteDelete on the world.
    mobrpg catalog <world> <kind> [--size N] [--names-only] [--json]
                                  List the elements of one kind (names + ids),
                                  e.g. political/type, landfeature, person. See
                                  what exists before pushing, to avoid dup Types.
    mobrpg map <init|sync|check> <world> --vault <path> [--map FILE]
                                  Generate/maintain the per-vault type mapping.
                                  init: discover mobRPG types + scan vault vocab ->
                                  propose _meta/mobrpg-map.json. sync: re-discover +
                                  merge non-destructively (preserve confirmed).
                                  check: coverage report. Read-only on mobRPG.
    mobrpg pull-canon <world> --vault <path> [--no-verify] [--execute]
                                  Pull ratified mobRPG canon down into vault
                                  mobrpg: nodes. mobRPG is canon; the vault is
                                  the working surface: accepted fills ids,
                                  accepted-after-edit/drift overwrites
                                  `determined`, dismissed records a note,
                                  deleted flags, pending leaves the vault alone.
                                  Verifies each accepted element to detect drift
                                  and deletion; --no-verify skips that pass.
    mobrpg pull-desc <world> --vault <path>
                    [--resolve take-canon|keep-vault|merge|separate|baseline
                     --only <ref>] [--execute]
                                  Reconcile note *description prose* with mobRPG
                                  canon (pull-canon only handles the machine
                                  node). No flags = read-only report classifying
                                  each synced note (in-sync / canon-only /
                                  vault-only / conflict) with diffs. --resolve
                                  applies one entity's outcome via --only: take-
                                  canon, keep-vault, merge (diff3 — leaves conflict
                                  markers on overlap), separate (stop reconciling
                                  it), baseline (first-time base capture). Change
                                  detection compares mobRPG's raw HTML hash and the
                                  frozen canon base, never the lossy converter.
                                  Vault-only writes, gated by --execute (dry-run
                                  default). The mobrpg-sync skill is the interactive
                                  UI; it walks the GM through conflicts one at a time.
    mobrpg suggest-desc <world> --vault <path> [--only <ref>] [--threshold F]
                        [--batch-label L] [--execute]
                                  Suggest a linked note's authored *description*
                                  UP to mobRPG when the element is an empty stub
                                  or poorer than the vault (the inverse of pull-
                                  desc). Emits one UpdateElement suggestion per
                                  candidate (sparse edit: targetRef=real element
                                  id + the canon-section prose as HTML) — a
                                  reviewable proposal the world owner accepts,
                                  never a direct element PUT. Candidate = the live
                                  description is empty, or differs below
                                  --threshold similarity (difflib, autojunk off;
                                  default 0.98); --only targets one note. Dry-run
                                  by default; --execute submits (prod also needs
                                  MOBRPG_ALLOW_PROD_WRITES=1). Each edit carries a
                                  distinct `<ns>:desc/<relpath>` externalRef so a
                                  re-run dedupes instead of piling up.
    mobrpg relink --vault <path> --to <new-rel-path> [--from <old-rel-path>]
                  [--execute]
                                  Re-point a moved/renamed note's mobrpg
                                  external_ref to its current path, keeping
                                  element_id and recording the old ref in
                                  previous_ref. Vault-only (no API); run after
                                  renaming/moving a synced note so a re-push
                                  won't mint a duplicate. Dry-run without
                                  --execute; --from guards against relinking the
                                  wrong note.
    mobrpg backfill <world> --vault <path> --crosswalk <file.json>
                    [--namespace NS] [--execute]
                                  One-time: migrate a sidecar crosswalk into
                                  per-entity mobrpg: nodes. Offline (no API
                                  calls); only needed for a vault that kept IDs
                                  in a separate crosswalk file instead of node
                                  frontmatter.
    mobrpg update <world> <suggestionId> <update.json> [--execute]
                                  Replace a Pending suggestion's payload (PUT,
                                  whole-payload). Edits inline fields only (name,
                                  description, altNames, data e.g. Person.languages)
                                  — cannot add classifier edges; use submit-batch.
    mobrpg review <world> <suggestionId> <accept|dismiss|reinstate>
                       [--note TEXT] [--execute]
                                  GM review action on one suggestion (needs
                                  ReadWriteDelete). Dry-run without --execute.
                                  accept is irreversible; dismiss keeps the
                                  externalRef claimed (see resubmit note below).
    mobrpg write <extract.json> <out_dir>
                                  Render a JSON extract into vault markdown.
    mobrpg merge <extract.json> <vault>
                                  Non-destructive merge for entities in both.
    mobrpg link-orphans <extract.json> <vault> <outdir>
                                  Auto-link obvious orphan relationships.
    mobrpg sync bootstrap <baseline_extract.json> <vault> <crosswalk.json>
    mobrpg sync sync <fresh_extract.json> <vault> <crosswalk.json> <report_dir>
                                  Detect mobRPG edits since the last pull.
    mobrpg push <world> --chapter <ch> [--execute] [--limit N] [--only S]
                                  Direct-create vault entities in mobRPG.
    mobrpg suggest <world> --chapter <ch> [--execute] [--limit N] [--only S]
                                  Submit vault entities as review suggestions
                                  (only needs Read access on the world). Emits
                                  bare CreateElements (no types/relationships).
    mobrpg submit-batch <world> <batch.json> [--execute]
                                  Submit a pre-built COMPOUND batch (classifier
                                  Types, Attribute edges, reified Event + Link
                                  relationships, in-batch ref/dependsOn wiring).
                                  Dry-run by default.
    mobrpg types <world> [--chapter <ch>] [--execute]
                                  Set entity types via Attribute edges.
    mobrpg links <world> --chapter <ch> [--execute] [--limit N]
                                  Push vault relationships as mobRPG events.
    mobrpg images <world> <vault> <crosswalk.json> [--execute]
                                  Pull entity images into the vault.

Mutating verbs are dry-run by default; add --execute to actually write. Run
`mobrpg <command> --help` for a command's own options.

## Typical workflows

Import a world into a vault:
    export MOBRPG_TOKEN=...            # read-only, prod is fine
    mobrpg pull <worldId> --out extract.json
    mobrpg write extract.json /path/to/vault
    mobrpg merge extract.json /path/to/vault
    mobrpg link-orphans extract.json /path/to/vault ./orphan_out

Push a vault chapter to mobRPG (safe: suggestions):
    export MOBRPG_TOKEN=...
    mobrpg suggest <worldId> --chapter chapter-2            # dry-run
    MOBRPG_ALLOW_PROD_WRITES=1 mobrpg suggest <worldId> --chapter chapter-2 --execute

Confirm the round-trip after a GM accepts them in mobRPG (read-only):
    mobrpg catalog <worldId> political/type                # see existing Types first
    mobrpg suggestions <worldId> --state Accepted --correlate --vault /path/to/vault
                                                            # externalRef -> vault file -> new element id
